head.hi: The Secretary of State to Ambassador O’Brien.
head.note: Repeated to Peking, Paris, Berlin, and St. Petersburg.

div.p: Mr. Knox instructs Mr. O’Brien to take an early opportunity of communicating formally to the foreign office the substance of the memorandum to the British foreign office which was embodied in the department’s telegram of November 6, taking care to set forth fully the ideas therein contained. Says that the Government of Great Britain has signified its approval of the general principle involved, and directs that he endeavor to secure a like favorable consideration of the project by the Government to which he is accredited.
